Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search emails for ExpenseBot rollout and pre-deployment communications using search_emails (query: "expense auto-categorizer" or "May 5" or "rollout" or "ExpenseBot" or similar). Find: (a) Samira Tariq's email to the eng team (email_samira_eng_rollout_deadline_0425) confirming the May 5 deadline for BrightLoop Analytics, Canopy Health, and GreenStack Energy with specific work items: Lena on pipeline hardening ENG-217, Dmitri on edge case test suite ENG-212, Anh on dashboard type validation ENG-218. Also mentions BrightLoop's HR contact Tessa Moreno asking when the stipend platform will be active. (b) Dmitri Volkov's reply (email_dmitri_test_failures_reply_0425) reporting 4 of 6 edge case tests passing, 2 failing: Test 4 excluded_categories as empty array falls through to default template values (same failure pattern as original Mosaic incident), and Test 6 max_amount as string causes TypeError in strict type enforcement. Also flags Jordan Ekwueme's BrightLoop stipend on admin hold from the fraud investigation (STIP-2026-0097). (c) Marcus Thorne's email (email_marcus_stipend_holds_brightloop_0425) about admin holds on 3 stipend transactions: Kevin Tran/Vectral (STIP-2026-0089), Andre Washington/Mosaic (STIP-2026-0094), Jordan Ekwueme/BrightLoop (STIP-2026-0097). Tessa Moreno did not confirm or deny Jordan's receipt she only asked when the reimbursement would be processed. Raises concern about new BrightLoop submissions flowing through the automated pipeline while the hold is unresolved.
OE 2: Search Slack for engineering team discussions about the rollout using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "expense" or "dashboard" or "pipeline" or "rollout" or "type mismatch" or similar). Find: (a) Anh Nguyễn's post in #engineering (channel C003, ts 1777160700.000000) identifying a blocker: the dashboard config editor (ENG-218) saves max_amount values as strings from HTML form inputs, but Lena's pipeline hardening code (ENG-217) rejects those configs with a TypeError during numeric comparison. This blocks the ops workflow for creating/updating client policy configs through the admin dashboard Chloe's team uses the dashboard for all policy management, so this blocks the rollout. (b) Samira Tariq's morning Slack in #engineering (channel C003, ts 1777212900.000000) reporting that Elena confirmed the May 5 deadline is firm. Elena also brought up the invoice attachment safety ticket it resurfaced during the Sunbelt and Palmetto cross-contamination postmortem that David ran last week. Elena asked whether engineering has done anything about it since March. Samira says it has been in backlog and Elena was not happy. Samira asks Lena to assess whether the invoice safety ticket should be pulled into rollout scope as a blocker or remain in backlog with a defensible justification, and requests a consolidated status on pipeline hardening, Dmitri's test results, and Anh's dashboard issue before noon.
OE 3: Search Linear for Expense Auto-Categorization project issues using linear_list_issues (query: "expense" or "pipeline hardening" or similar, or project: "proj_expense_autocategorizer"). Find: (a) ENG-217 (issue_pipeline_hardening): "[P1] Harden policy ingestion pipeline remove silent fallback, add type enforcement" assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, state in_progress, priority 1, due 2026-03-20 (overdue). (b) ENG-212 (issue_edge_cases_backlog): "Remaining edge case coverage policy config validation & malformed data handling" assigned to moveops_dmitri_volkov, state in_progress, priority 2, due 2026-03-20 (overdue). (c) ENG-218 (issue_dashboard_type_validation): "Dashboard: Add type validation to policy config editor" assigned to moveops_anh_nguyen, state in_progress, priority 2, due 2026-03-21 (overdue). (d) #187 (linear_issue_6b59cce3afc2): "Invoice email template safety checks prevent attachment mismatches" assigned to moveops_lena_bjorkstrom, state backlog, priority 2, due 2026-04-18 (overdue). This is the ticket that came up in Elena's conversation with Samira.
OE 4: Read Linear comments on the pipeline hardening ticket using linear_list_comments (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening"). Find Anh Nguyễn's comment (comment_anh_frontend_backend_desync_0425) flagging the frontend-backend desync the dashboard config editor (ENG-218) saves max_amount values as strings, the pipeline ingestion (ENG-217) rejects them with a TypeError. This blocks the ops workflow for creating/updating client policy configs. Needs resolution before the wider rollout to BrightLoop, Canopy, and GreenStack.
OE 5: Search emails for the Sunbelt/Palmetto invoice cross-contamination incident and the invoice safety ticket history using search_emails (query: "invoice" or "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or "attachment" or similar). Find: (a) Samira Tariq's email to Marcus and David (CC: lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com) acknowledging that the invoice attachment safety ticket was opened after the Terraform pricing exposure, Lena pushed it back on April 6 for DOT compliance work, and Samira allowed it to remain in backlog without an assignee. The current Sunbelt/Palmetto problem is the second incident touching the same failure mode no pre-send check that attached PDF matches the email recipient, no filename/customer-name mismatch warning. (b) Marcus Thorne's closeout expectations email stating this was a control failure, not a one-off send mistake requiring corrected documentation for both Palmetto and Sunbelt, and reactivation of the dormant invoice safety engineering work.
OE 6: Search Slack for additional context on the invoice safety ticket using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "invoice safety" or "attachment mismatch" or "Sunbelt" or "Palmetto" or similar) or retrieve #engineering (C003) and #finance (C005) history using conversations_history. Find: (a) Lena Björkström's own April 7 message in #engineering (C003, ts 1775520600.000000) stating she is pushing back the invoice safety ticket for DOT compliance work ticket is still unassigned, would need Samira to reshuffle priorities. (b) Samira's message in #engineering (C003, ts 1776345000.000000) explicitly connecting the Sunbelt/Palmetto incident to the same attachment-mismatch failure mode as the Terraform leak the second pricing-exposure incident. (c) Marcus Thorne's message in #finance (C005, ts 1776957000.000000) putting a hard line on this: demanding engineering reopen and actively assign the attachment-mismatch safeguard work, stating "we are not documenting it a second time without shipping a control."
OE 7: Search emails for stipend fraud investigation and admin hold details using search_emails (query: "stipend" or "fraud" or "hold" or "STIP" or "receipt" or similar). Find: (a) Dmitri Volkov's email to Marcus reporting 3 suspicious stipend submissions with forensic similarities Kevin Tran ($89 keyboard, Vectral), Andre Washington ($89 keyboard, Mosaic), Jordan Ekwueme ($45 monitor stand, BrightLoop) all from vendor TechComfort Supply, Kevin and Andre sharing identical receipt SHA-256 hashes. (b) Marcus Thorne's responses confirming admin holds: STIP-2026-0089 (Kevin Tran, $89, already paid), STIP-2026-0094 (Andre Washington, $89, held), STIP-2026-0097 (Jordan Ekwueme, $45, held). Marcus's email to Emeka requesting verification from Tamara Byrd (Mosaic) and Tessa Moreno (BrightLoop). This context is critical because the BrightLoop hold on Jordan Ekwueme directly affects the May 5 rollout new submissions will flow through automation while the old hold is unresolved.
OE 8: Send email to Samira with consolidated pre-deployment status using send_email (sender: "lena.bjorkstrom@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"], cc: ["dmitri.volkov@moveops.com", "anh.nguyen@moveops.com"], subject: related to "ExpenseBot Pre-Deployment Status" or "May 5 Rollout Readiness Assessment" or similar). Content must cover: (a) Pipeline hardening (ENG-217) status type enforcement and fallback removal implemented, but 2 of Dmitri's 6 edge case tests failing (Test 4: empty array fallback, Test 6: string type rejection). (b) Dashboard-backend type mismatch blocker Anh's ENG-218 dashboard saves max_amount as strings, Lena's ENG-217 pipeline rejects them, blocking ops config workflow. (c) Dmitri's edge case test suite (ENG-212) results 4 of 6 passing, 2 failing against the pipeline hardening branch. (d) Invoice safety ticket (#187) still in backlog since March, now urgent after Sunbelt/Palmetto cross-contamination postmortem; Elena asked about it specifically; needs decision on whether it's a rollout blocker or can remain in backlog with justification. (e) BrightLoop stipend hold timing conflict Marcus has admin holds on 3 fraud investigation transactions including Jordan Ekwueme (STIP-2026-0097) from BrightLoop; new submissions will flow through automation while the hold is unresolved; Tessa Moreno has not confirmed receipt verification.
OE 9: Post a message in #engineering Slack channel using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: consolidated rollout readiness summary covering all identified blockers and risks for the May 5 deadline). Content should include: the 2 failing edge case tests, the dashboard-backend type mismatch, the invoice safety ticket status and Elena's concern, and the BrightLoop stipend hold timing risk.
OE 10: Create a Linear comment on the pipeline hardening ticket (ENG-217) using linear_create_comment (issueId: "issue_pipeline_hardening", body: documenting the consolidated blocker list the 2 failing tests, the dashboard desync, and the dependency chain between ENG-217, ENG-212, and ENG-218 that must be resolved before May 5) OR create a new Linear issue using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title: related to "ExpenseBot May 5 Rollout Blockers" or similar, description: consolidated list of all blockers, priority: 1) to track the rollout readiness blockers in one place.
OE 11: Look up Samira Tariq's email address using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq" or "Samira") or contacts_get_contacts to confirm samira.tariq@moveops.com before sending the email in OE 8. Also look up Dmitri Volkov and Anh Nguyễn if needed for CC.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
